Core question: What exactly are A/B/C/D doing over the Yang cycle, and how are source table durations normalized?
Expected deliverables: Machine-readable manifest; source-to-normalized schedule table; pressure-class metadata; label glossary.
Boundaries / non-goals: No solver edits; no tank/header model; no event policy beyond fixed durations.

Core question: Which two named beds are passed into existing paired-bed machinery for every direct transfer?
Expected deliverables: Pair-map schema; donor/receiver/end metadata; EQI/EQII/PP/PU/AD&PP/BF validators.
Boundaries / non-goals: No new physics; no row-order inference; no implicit shared manifolds.

Core question: How are state_A/state_B/state_C/state_D stored, selected, and updated across slots?
Expected deliverables: Named state container; state injection/extraction contract; terminal-state writeback rules; initialization policy.
Boundaries / non-goals: Do not reset non-participating beds; do not store internal transfer tanks as physical state.

Core question: How does the wrapper make toPSAil see a normal paired or single-bed problem?
Expected deliverables: Case-builder functions; local/global bed mapping; native step translation; boundary/end setup; regression protection.
Boundaries / non-goals: Do not modify core adsorber equations; do not assemble [A,B,C,D] into one RHS.

Core question: How are external metrics and CSS reconstructed across the whole four-bed cycle?
Expected deliverables: External/internal component ledger; Yang-style purity/recovery definitions; all-bed CSS residuals; output metadata.
Boundaries / non-goals: Do not count internal transfers as product; do not claim Yang validation before mismatch register is complete.
